mappings.rationaleAI: Core VSI for this section, covering cell theory, cell classification (procaryotic vs. eucaryotic), cell membrane, nuclear envelope, organelles, the cell cycle, and cell division.
Covers chromosomes, DNA replication, cell division (mitosis and meiosis), and molecular biology relevant to B.1.a (nucleic acids, proteins) and D (the cell cycle).
Addresses macromolecules in cells (nucleic acids, proteins) and the molecular underpinnings of genetic replication and cell design (B.1).
Covers the cell as a molecular system (B.1) including biochemical reactions, DNA, proteins, and small molecules like lipids and amino acids (B.1.b).
Relevant to cell macromolecules and small molecules (B.1), covering chemical bonds, alpha helices in proteins, and molecular recognition in cellular processes.
Addresses cell differentiation, cells in combination forming tissues and multicellular organisms (C.5), and fertilization events (E).
Directly relevant to the cell cycle (D), cell division, and cells in combination forming tissues (C.5).
Covers the tools essential for studying cell morphological elements (B.2.b) including electron microscopy for organelles, ribosomes, and cytoskeleton.
Covers procaryotic cell structure and function (A.2.a, B.2.c), illustrating the contrast between procaryotic and eucaryotic cells.
Addresses procaryotic and eucaryotic microorganisms (A.2.a, B.2.c), including bacteria, archaea, and fungi at the cellular level.
Covers outstanding features of the plant cell and plant cell types (A.2.c) including meristematic, epidermal, and other specialized plant cells.
Addresses proteins as functional molecules in cells (B.1.a) and the enzymatic processes central to cell metabolism and the cell cycle.
